title: Cut Wave 3 task 3.6 (install-precommit-hooks.sh) — document 3-line snippet instead
body: Pre-commit hook is adopter ergonomics; A10 already catches the leak vector at PR-review per the risk register. Document the 3-line `git config core.hooksPath` snippet in 3.7 instead. Ship the script post-merge if anyone asks. NOTE: contradicts Q1 (Justin chose to ship script + docs); this trim is scope-discipline's recommendation only.

title: A1.5 disagreement-path test — exercise tampered fixture, assert non-zero exit
body: Wave 3.5 doesn't exercise the disagreement path. Add a tampered-fixture sub-test asserting non-zero exit + --best-effort downgrade.

title: e12 fresh-install needs explicit DOM test scenario
body: Task 3.2 mentions empty-state banner generically but doesn't enumerate 'no persona-rankings.jsonl exists at all' as a distinct DOM-test scenario. Add 'no JSONL + roster.js present → empty-state banner + (never run) rows' sub-case in Wave 3.2.

title: Path validation breaks tests under /tmp or repo paths
body: validate_project_root() rejects non-absolute paths and paths outside $HOME, but tests often use fixture-relative or /tmp paths. A3/scan-confirmation tests need to pass absolute fixture paths under the repo, or set MONSTERFLOW_ALLOWED_ROOTS explicitly. Document in plan task 1.11.

title: Dashboard banner assertions: CSS class + load-bearing word, not full copy equality
body: Otherwise A5 is brittle to whitespace edits. Assert .banner-privacy / .banner-empty-state CSS class + one load-bearing word.

title: Cost↔value-window join still undefined — declare honest two-signal split
body: Plan windows over docs/specs/<feature>/<gate>/ artifact directories on value side, but cost walk emits (persona, gate, parent_session_uuid). No stable key linking parent session UUID to a feature artifact directory. total_tokens, avg_tokens_per_invocation, cost_only look artifact-windowed when they aren't. Fix: declare two honestly separated signals in v1 — value metrics windowed over 45 artifact directories; cost metrics windowed over 45 observed Agent dispatches per (persona, gate), explicitly NOT aligned to value-window. Dashboard tooltip explains they're different windows. v1.1 captures join keys.

title: tmux non-tty refusal will hit Justin day-one — add --confirm-scan-roots flag
body: dev-session.sh pipes claude window output to a session log file. Under tmux pipe-pane, isatty(stdin) returns False. First-time --scan-projects-root ~/Projects from Justin's actual workflow will silently refuse, log 'scan-roots not confirmed; skipping K roots,' and never populate scan-roots.confirmed. Fix: add --confirm-scan-roots <dir> non-interactive flag; document in docs/persona-ranking.md; emit self-diagnostic stderr message when refusing.

title: Merge 7 test files → 4 — combine privacy-side + output-side enforcement
body: Combine test-finding-id-salt.sh + test-scan-confirmation.sh + test-path-validation.sh → test-privacy-gates.sh (all privacy-side); fold test-no-raw-print.sh into test-allowlist.sh (both output-side enforcement).

title: Strengths called out: allowlist privacy gate + file:// sidecar + counts-only telemetry + dashboard-as-passive-renderer
body: Codex 'What I'd Keep' notes these four design choices as good. Wave-level DoD lines double as graduation checklists for /build. run_state 6-state table is excellent test contract for A2. A8 idempotency contract (sort_keys=True + round(x,6) + diff-stable allowlist) is gold-standard.

title: Salt perms test uses python3 os.stat for portability (not stat -f vs -c)
body: Cross-platform via Python's os.stat instead of macOS-vs-Linux stat differences.

title: test-no-raw-print.sh regex needs pinning — positive/negative corpus + scope
body: What regex catches print(...) but not safe_log(...) or comments? Pin regex shape + positive/negative corpus + scope (only compute-persona-value.py, not the redaction CLI).

title: A4 overstates content-hash reset enforceability — reword as product decision
body: The spec admits historical dispatches lack persona-content hashes, but A4 still asserts pre-edit contributing_finding_ids[] are cleared. Without per-dispatch hash capture, you can only clear drill-down by intentionally discarding all prior rows for the current persona hash. Reword A4: 'after persona edit + one fresh dispatch, contributing_finding_ids[] for the new hash contains only post-edit IDs (pre-edit IDs are dropped wholesale at hash-change detection time).'

title: Content-hash 90%-pre-edit-data tooltip — show 'Hash recently changed' badge
body: 'Best-effort' content-hash window reset can show 90% pre-edit data for up to 44 invocations after a persona edit. Persona authors will draw wrong conclusions from mixed-window rates. Add 'Hash recently changed' tooltip/badge when current hash doesn't match the oldest row's hash in window.

title: Collapse 11 dashboard columns → 7 — move hash + last_seen to tooltip
body: Cut total_tokens (redundant with avg per decision #20), persona_content_hash and last_artifact_created_at (move to tooltip on persona name), merge runs_in_window + run_state badge into 'Coverage: 14/18 complete' column. Same JSONL data emitted; less render clutter.

title: A0 spike-result content checks — assert wc -l > 10 + literal token checks
body: test-phase-0-artifact.sh only asserts file existence; add wc -l > 10 + literal token checks (total_tokens, subagents/agent-, verdict line) so a 1-line TODO can't pass A0.

title: Salt file mid-run corruption / partial-write unspecified — validate on read + atomic write
body: Zero-byte salt collapses entropy to public hash; truncated salt drops to 64 bits silently; concurrent first-run race produces orphaned IDs in JSONL. Fix: validate on read (len == 32 bytes, non-zero); atomic write via tmp + os.replace on first generation; regenerate-and-clear-rankings-JSONL on validation failure (drill-down continuity reset is the only honest behavior).

title: Task 1.5 (value walk) under-declares dep on 0.4 fixtures
body: Listed deps '1.1, 1.2' — but 1.5 walks findings.jsonl/survival.jsonl/raw/ per artifact directory and is undevelopable without 0.4's cross-project fixtures. Add 0.4 to 1.5's 'Depends On'. Compare with 1.3, which correctly declares 0.5 for the same reason.

title: install.sh README discoverability — file issue NOW for MONSTERFLOW_DEBUG_PATHS
body: Genuine deferral, but file the issue NOW so MONSTERFLOW_DEBUG_PATHS=1 is discoverable on day one. Surface in stderr telemetry message when paths would help but env var isn't set.

title: leakage-fail.jsonl inverted assertion needs meta-runner contract
body: A test that fails when run alone is brittle — a crash falsely 'passes' the inverted assertion. Fix: tests/test-allowlist.sh runs regular fixtures (which pass); separate tests/test-allowlist-inverted.sh invokes validator with leakage-fail.jsonl, asserts both non-zero exit code AND specific stderr violation message (e.g., additionalProperties: 'finding_title'). Only second is allowed to fail successfully.

title: Silent personas misclassified as 'never run' — must read participation.jsonl
body: Existing findings-emit@1.0 deliberately writes participation.jsonl rows for personas that ran but emitted nothing (status: ok, findings_emitted: 0). Plan keys 'data row' off findings.jsonl personas[] and '(never run)' off roster-minus-data — silent personas fall through both filters and render as 'never run' when they actually ran. Fix: add silent_runs_count to row schema; state machine adds 7th state 'silent' between complete_value and roster-only; compute-persona-value.py reads participation.jsonl per artifact directory. Dashboard renders silent badge distinct from 'never run' badge.

title: A1 + A8 acceptance criteria lack named Wave 3 verification tasks
body: Wave 1 task 1.3 builds the cost walk; 1.4 covers A1.5; task 3.1 only enumerates A2/A3/A4/A7/A11. A1's 'sum(per_persona_tokens) == sum(usage rows)' assertion isn't bound to any named test. A8 idempotent-refresh similarly lacks a verification task. Fix: add A1 + A8 to 3.1's enumerated list (same tests/test-compute-persona-value.sh file).

title: --list-projects violates counts-only-telemetry privacy model — fold into --dry-run
body: CLI promises --list-projects, while telemetry is counts-only and paths are interactive-only/debug-only. Project list without paths is useless; path list violates steady-state privacy rule. Fix: fold --list-projects into --dry-run (also satisfies scope-discipline S2). One fewer flag; same use case.

title: A1.5 calibration based on one fixture (RedRabbit) — re-run pre-release
body: Q1 probe was one user, one machine, one Claude Code version — under-calibrated for cache-state / SDK-version field semantics. Re-run A1.5 against one additional project + version before public release. Becomes a pre-release smoke check (post-/build, pre-merge).

title: session-cost.py cannot be imported as session_cost — hyphen in filename
body: Plan decision #14 + Δ6 + spec §Integration assume `from session_cost import PRICING, entry_cost` works via sys.path insert. It doesn't — hyphens are illegal in Python module names. Verified: `python3 -c 'from session_cost import x'` from scripts/ fails. Fix: importlib.util.spec_from_file_location('session_cost', 'scripts/session-cost.py') (3 lines, one-time at top of compute-persona-value.py). This is a planning-stage hallucination caught by Codex by reading the actual filename rather than the spec text.

title: Color band assertions: CSS class boundaries (.band-low/.band-mid/.band-high), not RGB pixel diff
body: Visual color-band thresholds testable via class presence; pixel diff is brittle.

title: Defer --explain PERSONA[:GATE] to v1.1 — duplicates dashboard drill-down
body: Nothing in A0–A11 requires --explain. Population of one. Defer to v1.1.

title: jsonschema is an undeclared Python dependency — repo is stdlib-only
body: Plan decision #1 + scalability persona's 'stdlib only' constraint conflict with the planned jsonschema.validate(row, schema) per row. Verified: python3 -c 'import jsonschema' → ModuleNotFoundError on this machine. Fix: implement narrow allowlist validator inline (~30 lines of python — checks additionalProperties: false + required[] + enum/pattern for the actual fields). Don't add a Python dep on public-release week.

title: Deliberate-failure fixture needs test-runner isolation — separate expected-failure subtest
body: leakage-fail.jsonl is supposed to fail 'when run alone' — normal tests/run-tests.sh cannot include it in the same pass path unless allowlist test explicitly excludes it, then invokes a separate expected-failure subtest. Spell that out.

title: Task 3.7 docs over-declares dep on 3.1 — only needs 1.1 + 1.6 + 3.6
body: Documentation depends on implementations existing, not test results. Honest dep is 1.1 + 1.6 + 3.6, not 3.1 + 3.6. As written, 3.7 idles waiting for the largest test task instead of shipping in parallel with 3.1–3.5.
